entries.summary: Unit 42 documented 'phantom squatting': LLMs consistently hallucinate web domains for legitimate brands, and adversaries register those nonexistent domains to intercept AI-generated traffic. Across 913 brands and 685,339 queries they found 13,229 malicious URLs and ~250,000 still-unregistered hallucinated domains — and predicted adversary registrations 18–51 days ahead.
entries.takeaway: LLM hallucinations are a predictable supply-chain attack surface: attackers pre-register the domains/packages models invent.

lessons.point: Hallucinated domains/packages are predictable — defenders can enumerate and pre-register or block them before attackers do
lessons.excerpt: predict use of these domains from 18-51 days ahead of adversary registration

lessons.point: AI coding assistants are being used to build the phishing kits that then exploit hallucinated targets
lessons.excerpt: an attacker who leveraged an AI coding assistant to build a full phishing kit named Montana Empire

actionable.title: Enumerate & monitor your brand's hallucinated domains
actionable.detail: Query LLMs for your brand's URLs/packages at scale, then pre-register or block-list the hallucinated ones and monitor for adversary registration.

entries.title: Phantom Squatting: attackers register the domains LLMs hallucinate
@redhat-cloud-services npm namespace compromise (32+ packages)
entries.threat: Attackers compromised at least 32 packages under the @redhat-cloud-services scope, bypassing code review to push a payload dubbed Miasma.
entries.conditions: Affects consumers of the affected scoped packages and downstream builds that pulled compromised versions.
entries.mitigations: Identify affected versions from the Red Hat advisory; pin to clean releases; rotate impacted credentials; verify package signatures/provenance; harden publishing with mandatory review and 2FA.

generated_note: Curated entries are hand-reviewed. Auto-discovered entries (needs_review=true) are appended by scripts/aggregate.py from credible source feeds and should be triaged before promotion.
